title: PM KUSUM Yojana
meta_description: PM KUSUM Yojana provides solar pumps and solar power plants for farmers with 60% subsidy from central and state governments.

hero.one_liner: Solar pumps and solar power plants for farmers with 60% government subsidy — reduce electricity costs and earn from surplus power

what_is_it: PM Kisan Urja Suraksha evam Utthaan Mahabhiyan (PM-KUSUM) launched in 2019 aims to provide solar energy solutions to farmers. The scheme has three components: Component A (solar power plants on barren land), Component B (standalone solar pumps), and Component C (solarization of existing grid-connected pumps).

Under Component B (most popular), farmers get standalone solar water pumps of 2HP to 7.5HP capacity with 60% subsidy (30% central + 30% state). The farmer pays only 40% (can get bank loan for this). Under Component C, existing electric pumps are converted to solar, and farmers can sell surplus power to the grid and earn money.

eligibility.label: Who can apply
eligibility.value: Individual farmers, farmer groups, cooperatives, panchayats, FPOs

eligibility.label: For Component B
eligibility.value: Farmer must have cultivable land with water source (borewell/river/canal)

eligibility.label: Pump capacity
eligibility.value: Up to 7.5 HP standalone solar pump

eligibility.label: Subsidy
eligibility.value: 60% from government (30% central + 30% state), 40% farmer share

eligibility.label: Documents
eligibility.value: Aadhaar, land records, bank account, existing electricity connection details (for Component C)

how_to_apply.title: Visit your state's KUSUM portal
how_to_apply.description: Each state has its own portal for PM KUSUM registration. Search for '[Your State] PM KUSUM apply online'.

how_to_apply.title: Register and fill application
how_to_apply.description: Enter personal details, land details, existing irrigation setup, and requested pump capacity.

how_to_apply.title: State department verification
how_to_apply.description: State agriculture/energy department verifies your land records and eligibility.

how_to_apply.title: Pay your share and get solar pump
how_to_apply.description: After approval, pay your 40% share (bank loan available). The empaneled vendor installs the solar pump at your farm.

important_alert.text: PM KUSUM is implemented by state governments, so availability, subsidy percentage, and application process varies by state. Check your state's specific KUSUM portal for exact details and timelines.

important_dates.label: Application
important_dates.value: Depends on state — check state KUSUM portal

faqs.question: How much does a solar pump cost after subsidy?
faqs.answer: A 3HP solar pump costs approximately ₹2.5-3 lakh. With 60% subsidy, farmer pays ₹1-1.2 lakh. A 5HP pump costs around ₹3.5-4 lakh, farmer pays ₹1.4-1.6 lakh.

faqs.question: Can I earn money by selling solar power?
faqs.answer: Yes, under Component C. If you solarize your existing grid-connected pump, you can sell surplus power to the DISCOM and earn revenue. This makes your pump effectively free over time.

faqs.question: Is bank loan available for farmer's share?
faqs.answer: Yes. Banks provide loans for the farmer's 40% share at priority sector lending rates. The solar pump itself can serve as collateral.

faqs.question: What maintenance is required?
faqs.answer: Solar panels need minimal maintenance — just occasional cleaning. The vendor provides 5-year warranty on panels and 2-year on motor/pump. After warranty, maintenance cost is minimal (₹2,000-3,000/year).

ministry: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
